Your Opportunity Starts Here. 
Derivatives Support Officer 
Why Join The incumbent will be responsible for the post-execution support of new and existing Equities and rates derivative products, ensuring timely and accurate capture of trades into Bank of Singapore core banking risk system. This is also a control function role requiring the incumbent to work closely with Front Office, Trading Desks and Technology in managing the bank's risk relating to the supported product classes. These combined, the incumbent should be able to contribute significantly in a team setting, apply critical thinking to problem solving and excel under stressful conditions. 
What you do  Trade Capture and Trade Lifecycle management   
 Ensure accurate capture and processing of trades into Bank's core banking platform within regulatory timelines
 Monitor and manage STP exceptions to ensure completeness and accuracy of trade bookings
 Manage queries from internal and external stakeholders relating to trade bookings and /or trade lifecycle events.
 Perform independent assessment of lifecycle events and reconcile against counterparties
 Book lifecycle resultants into core banking for settlements and update of clients positions
   Controls   Perform End of Day control checks and ensure BAU tasks are completed
 Reconcile and investigate any inter-system breaks flagged escalate to the relevant parties where necessary
 Nostro and Stock Breaks investigation and resolution
 Perform daily validation and enrichment of derivatives reporting for MAS (DTCC) and HKMA (HKTR)
   Collateral management   Reconcile and manage BOS cash collateral with counterparties
 Liaise with risk team in the event of disputes arising from calling / top-ups of collateral
   Documentation and Reconciliation   Perform validation on long form ISDA confirmation for FX derivatives and Equities derivatives products received from counterparties against our trade booking in T24 core system
 Liaise with counterparties/internal stakeholders for any trade discrepancies
 Daily update tracker and monitoring of non-receipt of confirmation from counterparties and escalation where applicable
 Update weekly control report to ensure our documentation control aligned with KRI 
   Change Management   Identify control gaps and work with team to review process efficiency and deliver improvements
 Participate in project deliveries and UAT where nominated by line manager
   Ad Hoc Manage any ad hoc assignments delegated by line manager Who you are  
 3 to 5 years experience in Private Bank Operations, especially in the area of Equities Settlements or Equity Derivatives role.
 Clear written & verbal communication skills with all stakeholders, including Dealers, Front Office, and other support divisions
 Ability to work and deliver effectively under time pressure
 Working knowledge on industry platforms like Temenos or Triresolve is preferred
 Familiarity on derivatives regulatory reporting requirements and VBA is a plus
 Degree or Diploma qualified

This role offers you the chance to immerse yourself in the world of equity research, where your analytical acumen and passion for uncovering market insights will be highly valued. You will work alongside a knowledgeable team, leveraging advanced research techniques and data analysis to support investment decisions and drive impactful publications. The organisation is committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive workplace, providing flexible working opportunities and ongoing training to help you grow your career. You will be expected to keep pace with rapidly changing market conditions by continuously monitoring relevant news, economic indicators, and industry shifts. Success in this position requires not only technical proficiency but also strong interpersonal skills as you work closely with colleagues across functions. Your contributions will be integral to producing high-quality research reports that guide strategic decisions within the organisation. What you bring:To excel as an Equity Research Analyst, you will bring proven experience in applying analytical frameworks within finance or related fields. Your educational achievements reflect your commitment to learning, while your hands-on exposure-whether through internships or early career roles-demonstrates your ability to apply theory in practice. You possess exceptional quantitative skills complemented by a keen eye for detail, allowing you to construct reliable models that withstand scrutiny. Your fluency in English supports clear written communication across reports and presentations. Beyond technical expertise, your success will stem from your willingness to collaborate openly with colleagues, share knowledge generously, and adapt quickly as priorities shift. A genuine interest in financial markets paired with resilience under pressure ensures you remain engaged even during demanding periods. Company Profile

Tenity is a global innovation ecosystem and early-stage investor making innovation work and advancing the future of finance. 
Our story began back in 2015, when Tenity was founded in the heart of the Swiss financial center Zurich. Since then, we have grown to four hubs and a global team across Europe and Asia. We believe collaboration is the key to innovation. We bring together ambitious founders with leading corporations, forward-thinking investors, and passionate mentors – and together, we create an ecosystem that powers the future of finance. 

Job Description

Role overview: 
You will play an important role in the execution and delivery of the programs to support and accelerate the growth of startups’ journey. This will be an opportunity for you to learn, experience and interact with stakeholders in the ecosystem from founders to C-suite executives. If you are someone who is passionate about entrepreneurship, innovation with strong interest in ecosystem building and founder support. This is for you. 
Key Responsibilities: 

Responsible for supporting and contributing to the overall planning and execution of the programs conducted by Tenity 
Assist in program operations including event planning, scheduling and logistics (virtually/onsite) 
Help manage communication with founders, mentors and vendors 
Support in preparation of materials, reports and presentations 
Track programs KPIs and assist in post program evaluation 
Other Ad-hoc duties

Required skills: 

Enrolled in or recently graduated from University Curious about entrepreneurship and the latest technological and banking and financial services sector, and healthcare sector trends 
Have interest in Emerging technologies e.g. Generative AI, Fintech, Blockchain, Web3.0 and others 
Excellent organizational and communication skills 
Able to manage tasks independently and are a team player 
Loves getting deeply involved and derives huge satisfaction from working closely with startup founders 
Fluent in English, both spoken and written 
Familiar with Microsoft Office Suite, Zoom, Canva 

Administrative Requirements: 

Only candidates with necessary work permit and DO NOT require a visa sponsorship will be considered for the position.
Only candidates who are physically based in Singapore will be considered for the position
Team members are expected to report to office physically on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and other days where you are asked to be physically present for ad-hoc meetings and events

The success of your application also depends on the drive and passion you possess.
